rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly states it "extends justification effects to digital nudging," and its focal constructs include justification effects alongside assurance cues, indicating likely theoretical use rather than a passing mention. The cited 2005 JMR paper is a core justification-theory study on hedonic vs. utilitarian choice, which plausibly serves as a foundational mechanism source for that claim. The DOI match is exact (10.1509/jmkr.42.1.43.56889).
